operational_knowledge_units.title: HospitalDatabaseModel stores hospital queue and tote handoff locations
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The HospitalDatabaseModel table stores hospitals together with entrance and exit queue locations and the drop off and induction locations used for tote handoff behavior.
observed_evidence: Slide text states: 'The HospitalDatabaseModel table stores the hospitals and their entrance and exit queue location as well as the drop off and induction locations, this is where it drops off a tote and picks one up.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robotics Database Hospital slide: HospitalDatabaseModel stores hospitals, entrance queue location, exit queue location, drop off location, and induction location. These locations define where a tote is dropped off and picked up.

operational_knowledge_units.title: HosptialSorterSideDatabaseModel maps sorter sides to hospitals
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The HosptialSorterSideDatabaseModel table stores the association between sorter sides and their related hospital.
observed_evidence: Slide text states: 'The HosptialSorterSideDatabaseModel table stores the mapping of sortersides and its associated hospital.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robotics Database Hospital slide: HosptialSorterSideDatabaseModel stores mapping between sorter sides and the associated hospital.

operational_knowledge_units.title: CellCodeDatabaseModel stores map cell codes used for hospital lookup
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The CellCodeDatabaseModel stores all cell codes in the map and is used to determine what hospital a cell code is connected to.
observed_evidence: Slide text states: 'The CellCodeDatabaseModel stores all cell codes in the map.'
Slide text also states: 'we use the CellCode table to lookup what hospital its connected to'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robotics Database Hospital slide: CellCodeDatabaseModel stores all map cell codes and can be used to look up which hospital a cell code is connected to.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Running-state AGV add flow checks missing zone before hospital lookup and Geek initialize
operational_knowledge_units.summary: When a new AGV is added while the system is in a running state, the system first checks whether the AGV's current cell code is in a missing zone under the sorter. If it is not, the system uses the CellCode table to find the connected hospital and sends an Initialize command to Geek.
observed_evidence: Slide text states: 'When a new AGV is added to the system if we are in a running state we will first check if the AGVs current cell code is in a missing zone under the sorter.'
Slide text continues: 'If not we use the CellCode table to lookup what hospital its connected to and we send a Initialize command to Geek.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: When a new AGV is added in a running state, the system first checks whether the AGV current cell code is in a missing zone under the sorter. If not, it uses the CellCode table to look up the connected hospital and sends an Initialize command to Geek.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Initialize a newly added AGV during running state using cell code to hospital lookup
preconditions: A new AGV is being added to the system
The system is in a running state
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Text-only slide describing database tables and runtime behavior for adding a new AGV, including missing-zone check, cell code hospital lookup, and Initialize command to Geek.

steps.instruction: Check whether the newly added AGV's current cell code is in a missing zone under the sorter.
steps.expected_outcome: You determine whether the AGV is currently in a missing zone under the sorter.
steps.validation_check: The AGV current cell code has been evaluated against the missing-zone condition described on the slide.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet describing CellCodeDatabaseModel and new AGV behavior

steps.instruction: If the AGV current cell code is not in a missing zone under the sorter, use the CellCode table to look up the hospital connected to that cell code.
steps.expected_outcome: The hospital associated with the AGV current cell code is identified.
steps.validation_check: A hospital mapping is obtained from the CellCode table for the AGV current cell code.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet describing CellCode table lookup to connected hospital

steps.instruction: Send an Initialize command to Geek.
steps.expected_outcome: Geek receives the Initialize command for the newly added AGV context.
steps.validation_check: The Initialize command has been issued after hospital lookup.
steps.visual_region_hint: Final clause of CellCodeDatabaseModel bullet mentioning Initialize command to Geek

warnings: The slide only specifies the branch for when the AGV current cell code is not in a missing zone; it does not define what to do if it is in a missing zone.
The slide does not specify the interface, command format, or operator actions for sending the Initialize command.
procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Procedure candidate from Robotics Database Hospital slide: when adding a new AGV in running state, check whether the AGV current cell code is in a missing zone under the sorter. If not, use the CellCode table to look up the connected hospital and send an Initialize command to Geek.
